raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that searches for the system host name in /etc/hosts and prints the IP address in the first awk field. Use hostname command to get host name. Then grep for that in /etc/hosts, then awk '{print $1}'. Probably need to handle multiple matches? Just first. So: hostname | xargs -I{} awk -v host="{}" '$0 ~ host {print $1; exit}' /etc/hosts. Or simpler: awk -v host="$(hostname)" '$0 ~ host {print $1; exit}' /etc/hosts. Return that.
